what_it_does: Implements a reusable React tweaks panel and control library for in-browser prototypes, including a floating shell, a state hook, a family of form controls, and a postMessage host protocol for edit-mode activation and persistence.
observations: Defines a postMessage protocol with the host: listens for __activate_edit_mode / __deactivate_edit_mode, announces __edit_mode_available, and posts __edit_mode_set_keys / __edit_mode_dismissed.
Injects a large block of scoped CSS via a <style> tag rendered inside TweaksPanel.
TweaksPanel is draggable, clamps to viewport, and uses ResizeObserver with a window.resize fallback.
TweakRadio renders a draggable segmented control and falls back to a native select when option labels are too long.
TweakColor supports single-color or palette chips with a contrast-aware checkmark and falls back to <input type=color> when no options are given.
TweakNumber supports horizontal scrubbing on the label in addition to direct numeric input.
useTweaks dispatches a same-window tweakchange CustomEvent so in-page peers can react.
All exports are assigned to window as globals, implying script-tag usage.
standouts: Centralizes the host edit-mode activation/persistence protocol in one reusable module.
Segmented radio with automatic dropdown fallback based on label-length heuristics.
Color/palette chip picker with built-in luminance-aware checkmark.
Global window exports for drop-in HTML prototype usage.
conventions: React functional components with hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef).
CSS class namespace prefix twk-* for isolation.
Pointer events for drag and scrub interactions.
Global window assignment for UMD-like consumption without a module loader.
Comments explain protocol ordering and fallback behavior.
concerns: postMessage uses target origin '*', with no origin validation.
Drag and scrub listeners are attached to window and may leak if the component unmounts before pointerup/mouseup.
TweaksPanel header drag uses mouse events while scrub/radio use pointer events, creating inconsistency.
No local persistence of tweak values if the host is absent; state resets to defaults on reload.
High z-index 2147483646 may conflict with other overlays.
notes: A self-contained runtime tweaks UI layer that both renders controls and negotiates edit-mode lifecycle with an enclosing host via postMessage.
questions: What host implementation consumes the __edit_mode_* postMessage protocol?
How does the host parse and rewrite the /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/ block on disk?
Are React and Babel loaded via script tags, and how are multiple prototypes prevented from conflicting on window globals?
How does the host coordinate when multiple iframes or peers all announce __edit_mode_available?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file centralizes the host protocol and ships a consistent set of reusable controls, exactly as the header comment states.
apparent_themes: Host-guest runtime protocol for live editing
Defensive accessibility attributes like role and aria-checked
intention_signals: The file is trying to give HTML-based prototypes a drop-in, polished parameter-tweaking UI that talks to an external editor/host, so individual prototypes do not reimplement controls or activation logic.
novelty: The combination of a host edit-mode protocol, auto-fallback segmented radios, palette-aware color chips, and global exports in a single JSX file is distinct from a generic component library.
contribution: A self-contained tweaks UI layer with a documented host message contract and a set of control primitives that a fused system could keep for live parameter editing in browser-based prototypes.
summary_for_embedding: tweaks-panel.jsx is a React-based floating tweaks panel with reusable controls and a postMessage host protocol for activating, dismissing, and persisting runtime design parameters in HTML prototypes.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable, host-aware tweaks interface so prototypes can expose editable parameters without reimplementing controls or edit-mode lifecycle handling.

symbols.does: State hook for tweak values that broadcasts edits to the host and same-window listeners.
symbols.description: Initializes tweak state from defaults and returns a setter that accepts either a single key/value pair or an object of edits. It posts __edit_mode_set_keys to the parent window and dispatches a tweakchange CustomEvent so peers in the same page can react.

symbols.does: Floating, draggable panel shell that manages edit-mode visibility via host messages.
symbols.description: Listens for __activate_edit_mode and __deactivate_edit_mode messages, announces __edit_mode_available on mount, and renders a fixed-position panel with a draggable header, close button, and scrollable body. It clamps position to the viewport using ResizeObserver or a resize fallback.

symbols.does: Message event handler that toggles panel open state from host signals.
symbols.description: Inspects incoming postMessage data type and calls setOpen(true) for __activate_edit_mode or setOpen(false) for __deactivate_edit_mode.

symbols.does: Closes the panel and notifies the host it was dismissed.
symbols.description: Sets open to false and posts __edit_mode_dismissed to the parent window so the host toolbar toggle stays in sync; the host then echoes __deactivate_edit_mode to actually hide the panel.

symbols.does: Mouse drag handler for repositioning the panel.
symbols.description: Captures the initial pointer position and panel bounds, then attaches mousemove/mouseup listeners that update offsetRef and call clampToViewport to keep the panel inside the viewport.

symbols.does: Mousemove listener that updates panel offset during drag.
symbols.description: Computes new right/bottom offsets from pointer delta and applies them through clampToViewport.

symbols.does: Mouseup listener that cleans up drag event listeners.
symbols.description: Removes the mousemove and mouseup listeners registered by onDragStart.

symbols.does: Layout component that renders a section header.
symbols.description: Renders a styled uppercase label (twk-sect) and any following children, used to group related controls in the panel body.

symbols.does: Layout wrapper for a labeled control row.
symbols.description: Renders a label with optional value display and children; supports vertical or inline horizontal layout via the inline prop.

symbols.does: Range slider control for numeric tweaks.
symbols.description: Wraps a native range input in a TweakRow, formatting the current value with an optional unit and calling onChange with the numeric value.

symbols.does: Switch control for boolean tweaks.
symbols.description: Renders a button with role switch and aria-checked, toggling the value and calling onChange with the negated boolean.

symbols.does: Segmented radio control with optional select fallback.
symbols.description: Renders a draggable segmented button group when option labels fit a heuristic length, otherwise falls back to TweakSelect. It uses pointer events and a valueRef to avoid stale closures during drags.

symbols.does: Computes display length of a radio option label.
symbols.description: Returns the string length of an option's label property if the option is an object, otherwise the string length of the option value.

symbols.does: Maps a select string back to the original typed option value.
symbols.description: Finds the option whose string representation matches the selected value and returns its original value, preserving numbers, booleans, or object values.

symbols.does: Determines which segment is under a pointer coordinate.
symbols.description: Calculates the segment index from the pointer position within the track rectangle and clamps it to the valid option range.

symbols.does: Pointer-down handler that starts segment dragging.
symbols.description: Begins a drag, immediately selects the segment under the pointer, and attaches pointermove/pointerup listeners to update the value as the pointer moves across segments.

symbols.does: Pointer-move listener for segmented radio dragging.
symbols.description: Reads the segment under the current pointer position and calls onChange only when the hovered segment differs from the current valueRef value.

symbols.does: Pointer-up listener that ends segment dragging.
symbols.description: Sets dragging to false and removes the pointermove and pointerup listeners registered by onPointerDown.

symbols.does: Dropdown select control for option lists.
symbols.description: Renders a native select inside a TweakRow, supporting plain values or {value,label} option objects and emitting the string value on change.

symbols.does: Text input control for string tweaks.
symbols.description: Renders a styled text field inside a TweakRow and passes the input value to onChange on every change event.

symbols.does: Number input with horizontal scrub support.
symbols.description: Renders a labeled numeric input and allows scrubbing the label horizontally to adjust the value by step; clamps results to min/max and respects decimal precision.

symbols.does: Clamps a number to min and max bounds.
symbols.description: Returns min if n is below min, max if above max, otherwise n.

symbols.does: Pointer-down handler that starts number scrubbing.
symbols.description: Records the starting pointer x-coordinate and value, then attaches pointermove/pointerup listeners that compute a stepped, clamped value from horizontal movement.

symbols.does: Pointer-move listener for number scrubbing.
symbols.description: Calculates the raw value from pointer delta and step, snaps to step, clamps, and calls onChange with a fixed-decimal number.

symbols.does: Pointer-up listener that ends number scrubbing.
symbols.description: Removes the pointermove and pointerup listeners registered by onScrubStart.

symbols.does: Determines whether a hex color is light based on luminance.
symbols.description: Parses #rgb or #rrggbb hex strings, computes the YIQ relative luminance formula, and returns true if the weighted sum exceeds the light threshold, defaulting to true for invalid or non-hex input.

symbols.does: SVG checkmark icon for selected color chips.
symbols.description: Renders a small checkmark path whose stroke color adapts to the background lightness so it remains visible on both light and dark swatches.

symbols.does: Color/palette picker using chips or native color input.
symbols.description: When options are provided, renders a row of color chips that can represent single colors or palettes; selected chips show a contrast-aware checkmark. Without options it falls back to a native color input. Emits the option in its original shape.

symbols.does: Produces a lowercase string key for comparing color options.
symbols.description: Stringifies a color option with JSON.stringify, converts to lowercase, and handles undefined safely so comparisons against native color input values are case-insensitive.

symbols.does: Styled button control for actions.
symbols.description: Renders a primary or secondary button that invokes onClick, used for actions inside the tweaks panel.
